requirements.title: Written Disclosure Before AI Interaction
requirements.description: Healthcare entities must provide written disclosure to patients before any AI system interacts with or makes decisions about their care.

requirements.title: No Discriminatory AI Systems
requirements.description: AI systems must not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or age in clinical decisions.

requirements.title: Human Override Mechanism
requirements.description: All high-risk AI decisions must have a documented human review pathway. Clinicians must be able to override AI recommendations.

requirements.title: AI Decision Audit Trail
requirements.description: Hospitals must maintain logs of AI-assisted decisions for a minimum of 5 years, accessible to regulators within 30 days of request.

requirements.title: Vendor Contract Requirements
requirements.description: All AI vendor contracts must include data usage restrictions, bias testing attestations, and incident notification clauses.

penalties.description: Civil penalties up to $10,000 per violation per day

laws.notes: Signed into law 2025. Among the strictest state-level AI governance laws in the US.

laws.full_name: California AB 489 – Healthcare AI Disclosure Act

requirements.title: Prohibition on AI Impersonating Licensed Providers
requirements.description: AI systems must not represent themselves as licensed physicians, nurses, or other healthcare providers. Clear labeling required at all times.

requirements.title: Pre-Interaction AI Disclosure
requirements.description: Patients must be informed they are interacting with AI before interaction begins, including a plain-language description of what the AI does.

requirements.title: Patient Right to Opt Out
requirements.description: Patients have the right to opt out of AI-assisted care and receive human-only care pathways where clinically feasible.

penalties.description: Up to $10,000 per knowing violation; up to $25,000 for repeat violations

laws.notes: Specifically targets patient-facing AI in clinical settings.

requirements.title: AI Content Detection Tool Requirement
requirements.description: Any entity deploying generative AI in content-generating applications must provide a free AI detection tool to users.

requirements.title: AI-Generated Content Metadata
requirements.description: AI-generated clinical notes, reports, or patient communications must include metadata identifying them as AI-generated.

laws.notes: Applies broadly to any AI generating content, including clinical documentation AI.

laws.full_name: California AB 2013 – AI Training Data Transparency Act

requirements.title: Training Data Disclosure
requirements.description: AI developers must publish documentation about training data sources, including datasets used, data provenance, and known limitations.

laws.notes: Primarily affects AI vendors/developers. Hospitals should ensure their vendors comply.

requirements.title: Algorithmic Impact Assessment
requirements.description: Deployers of high-risk AI must conduct and document annual algorithmic impact assessments covering bias, accuracy, and fairness metrics.

requirements.title: Risk Management Program
requirements.description: Organizations must implement a documented AI risk management program aligned with NIST AI RMF or equivalent framework.

requirements.title: Opt-Out and Appeal Mechanism
requirements.description: Individuals must have the right to opt out of high-risk AI decisions and appeal adverse decisions made by or with AI assistance.

requirements.title: Deployer Notification to Developer
requirements.description: Deployers must notify AI developers of known or suspected algorithmic discrimination within 90 days of discovery.

requirements.title: AI System Inventory
requirements.description: Organizations must maintain a current inventory of all high-risk AI systems in use, updated at minimum annually.

penalties.description: Enforced by Colorado AG; civil penalties vary by violation type

laws.notes: Modeled after EU AI Act. Most comprehensive US state AI law. Healthcare is explicitly high-risk.

requirements.title: Annual AI Bias Audit
requirements.description: Employers using AI in employment decisions (including healthcare staffing AI) must conduct annual bias audits by independent third parties.

requirements.title: AI Use Notification
requirements.description: Organizations must notify individuals when AI is used in decisions that significantly affect them.

penalties.description: Up to $500 per violation per day

laws.notes: Focuses heavily on employment-related AI but extends to healthcare HR systems.

laws.full_name: New York City Local Law 144 – AI Hiring Bias Audit

requirements.title: Bias Audit for Automated Employment Decision Tools
requirements.description: NYC employers using AI in employment decisions must publish annual independent bias audit results.

requirements.title: Notice of Automated Decision Tool Use
requirements.description: NYC employers must notify candidates and employees when an automated employment decision tool is used to evaluate them.

penalties.enforcement_body: NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection

laws.notes: NYC-specific. One of the first-in-nation bias audit requirements.

laws.full_name: Florida HB 1459 – Government AI Transparency Act

requirements.title: Government AI Use Transparency
requirements.description: State agencies must publicly disclose all AI systems in use, their purpose, and accuracy metrics.

requirements.title: Patient Notification of AI in State-Funded Care
requirements.description: State-funded healthcare facilities must notify patients when AI systems are used in their care decisions.

laws.notes: Primarily affects state-funded healthcare (Medicaid, state hospitals).

laws.full_name: HIPAA with AI Addendum – HHS Guidance on AI and PHI

requirements.title: PHI Must Not Train AI Without Explicit Authorization
requirements.description: Protected Health Information may not be used to train AI models without explicit patient authorization. De-identification must meet Safe Harbor or Expert Determination standards.

requirements.title: Business Associate Agreement with AI Vendors
requirements.description: All AI vendors with access to PHI must have a signed and current Business Associate Agreement (BAA) in place.

requirements.title: Minimum Necessary PHI for AI Systems
requirements.description: AI systems must access only the minimum necessary PHI to perform their function. Bulk data access for AI training requires documented justification.

laws.notes: HHS issued formal guidance in 2024. Every US hospital must comply.

laws.full_name: CMS AI Strategy – Medicare/Medicaid AI Requirements

requirements.title: CDS AI Must Meet Evidence Standards
requirements.description: Clinical Decision Support AI tools used in Medicare/Medicaid settings must meet CMS evidence-based standards and be listed in approved tool registries.

requirements.title: Algorithm Transparency for Reimbursement AI
requirements.description: AI used in billing or prior authorization decisions for Medicare/Medicaid must provide explainable decision outputs auditable by CMS.

requirements.title: Equity Monitoring for CMS-Funded AI
requirements.description: Providers must monitor and report to CMS any evidence of health equity disparities in AI-assisted care delivery.

laws.notes: Applies to any provider accepting Medicare or Medicaid — virtually all US hospitals.

laws.full_name: HHS AI Safety Program – National Patient Safety Standards

requirements.title: Pre-Deployment Safety Testing
requirements.description: High-risk AI systems must undergo documented pre-deployment safety testing in clinical environments before production use.

requirements.title: AI Adverse Event Reporting
requirements.description: Hospitals must report AI-related adverse events to HHS within 30 days. Near-misses are strongly encouraged to be reported.

requirements.title: AI Governance Committee Required
requirements.description: Health systems with more than 100 beds must have a designated AI governance committee or officer responsible for oversight.

laws.notes: New program from HHS. Voluntary initially but expected to become mandatory for accreditation.

default.note: Federal laws apply to all US states. State-specific laws listed above for covered states.
